title: Unbounded `L1` causes out-of-bounds read when exporting `HybridCipher`
problem: `native_cipher->L1` is used directly as copy length for `ct_m` without enforcing `HYBRIDENCref_MAX_LEN`. If a device/library returns an oversized `L1`, JNI reads past the fixed `ct_m` buffer and copies invalid memory into Java.

title: Hybrid signature conversion can read past native buffer and pass invalid null arg
problem: `sig_m_len` is not bounded to `HYBRIDSIGref_MAX_LEN` before copy, so oversized lengths can read past `native_sig->sig_m`. Also, when `sig_m_len == 0`, `sig_m_array` stays `NULL`, but the new Java constructor requires non-null `sigM`, causing constructor failure.

title: Incomplete `l_value` validation allows invalid copy length in `java_to_native_HybridSignature_alloc`
problem: Only `l_value > HYBRIDSIGref_MAX_LEN` is checked. Negative values and values larger than `sig_m_array` length are not rejected before `GetByteArrayRegion`, which can produce runtime JNI exceptions and propagate inconsistent `native_sig->L`.

title: Removal of `L` fallback creates ECC cipher length regression
problem: `native_cipher->L` is now taken only from Java field `l`; when callers populate `c` but leave `l` at default 0 (legacy/common setter usage), JNI sends zero-length ciphertext to SDF even though bytes are present, causing decryption/import failures.
